why: Protocol 775 Configuration keep_alive runtime-send helper must send the same serverbound keep_alive frame that the official Configuration serverbound codec emits. This is the minimal outgoing helper prerequisite before a full spawn_reader keep-alive echo probe.
what: The exact Rust oracle test configuration_keepalive_runtime_send_helper_sends_official_configuration_frame fails because packet::send_keep_alive(&mut Conn { state: State::Configuration, protocol_version: 775 }, official input id) panics in Protocol 775 packet-id translation with 'bad packet state Play' before any complete keep_alive frame is observed on the localhost TcpListener. The helper is selecting the play serverbound keep_alive packet family instead of the Configuration serverbound keep_alive packet family.

answer.expected_network_shape: VarInt(length of official encoded_framed_hex bytes) followed by the official encoded_framed_hex bytes when Conn compression_threshold is -1

evidence.summary: Existing generated official answer from client.jar calls: ConfigurationProtocols.SERVERBOUND.codec().encode/decode, SERVERBOUND_TEMPLATE.details().listPackets, and ServerboundKeepAlivePacket.getId().
Runtime-send manifest declares the exact Rust oracle test and uses the existing official framed dispatch answer as the expected frame source.

Rust test reads the runtime manifest and existing answer, creates a localhost Conn in State::Configuration, calls packet::send_keep_alive with the official input id, and compares observed TcpStream bytes to the official framed payload with an outer network length prefix.
Current send_keep_alive chooses play::serverbound keep_alive packet structs for modern versions without branching on Conn state.

test_command: tmpdir=$(mktemp -d "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/rustmine-runtime-send-test.XXXXXX"); CARGO_TARGET_DIR="$tmpdir" cargo test --manifest-path oracle/rust-tests/Cargo.toml --test oracle_contracts configuration_keepalive_runtime_send_helper_sends_official_configuration_frame -- --exact; rc=$?; rm -rf "$tmpdir"; exit $rc
observed_failure: packet::send_keep_alive panicked in Configuration state before sending the official Protocol 775 Configuration serverbound keep_alive frame; underlying panic: bad packet state Play
